title: Sutro Baths and The Cliff House - Indecisive Traveler
content: Located in San Francisco, California, it was once a huge private swimming pool complex that was built in the late 19th century.

title: Sutro Baths - Extranomical Tours
content: Former San Francisco Mayor (1894-1896), and entrepreneur, Adolph Sutro opened his privately owned Sutro Baths to the public as the largest indoor swimming pool establishment in the world. The enclosure was built of glass, iron, wood and a reinforced concrete structure on a small beach inlet below the Cliff House, which Sutro also owned during the time. Today the Cliff House and Sutro Baths site are part of the Golden Gate National Recreation Area and operated by the United States National Park Service. ### Guided Alcatraz and San Francisco City Tour. This full-day guided tour breaks you into Alcatraz and explores the Palace of Fine Arts, Golden Gate Bridge, Land’s End and Twin Peaks. Visitors to the baths had a choice of 7 different swimming pools – one fresh water and 6 salt water baths ranging in temperature – a museum featuring Mr. Sutro’s large personal artifacts from his travels, a concert hall with seating for 8,000 and an ice skating rink.

title: Sutro Baths Question - San Francisco Forum - Tripadvisor
content: Sutro Baths was actually a natatorium--a large, palatial enclosure for swimming and water activities. It had water piped from the Pacific Ocean

title: Sutro Baths - San Francisco - Yelp
content: We had a lovely day at Sutro bath ruins where there is an amazing view and beautiful patch of calla lilies. It's quite a drive to get here but well worth it

title: Sutro Baths - All You SHOULD Know Before Going (2026 Reviews)
content: Located below the parking lot at the west end of Geary Street, at Point Lobos, the massive public bathhouse covered three acres and accommodated 10,000 bathers
